## 🌎 Geopolitics

### Hormuz Blockade Triggers India's Energy Pivot—Crude Now Priced in Yuan

The United States blockaded Iran's Strait of Hormuz in mid-April 2026 after failed peace talks, tightening global oil supplies just as India lost its waiver to purchase Russian crude at sea—a twin squeeze that expires April 11. In response, Indian refiners have restarted Iranian oil purchases for the first time in years, settling approximately 2 million barrel cargoes directly in Chinese yuan through ICICI Bank, bypassing dollar-denominated channels entirely. The shift signals New Delhi's tactical pivot away from US-dominated energy markets as geopolitical fracturing makes secure supply chains a strategic vulnerability.

**Why it matters.** For your consulting practice and market exposure, this is the real-time story of energy inflation driving Indian policy. Diesel prices jumped from ₹61.60 per litre on February 27 to ₹88.38 by April 22—a 43% spike in under two months. That bleeds directly into broader CPI expectations, rupee weakness (USDINR moves on energy imports), and corporate margin compression across logistics and manufacturing. The yuan settlement is India's clearest strategic autonomy move yet: it hedges dollar exposure during Trump tariff volatility while deepening BRICS currency experiments. For quant research, watch oil spreads (Brent-WTI differentials), INR volatility, and PSU refinery margins—this is the kind of geopolitical friction that moves correlations.

**Action.** Monitor India's May inflation print (CPI out mid-May) and RBI's forward guidance on fuel subsidies. Track USDINR vol into Q2 earnings season. Read CNBC's April 17 piece on US energy expansion plans—the US is simultaneously trying to grow LNG sales to India while blockading Iran, creating a structural contradiction that will shape bilateral ties through 2026.

*Sources: [cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/14/us-hormuz-blockade-hits-india-just-as-russian-oil-purchase-waiver-expires-deepening-energy-worries/) · [visiontimes.com](https://www.visiontimes.com/2026/04/21/indias-yuan-payment-for-iranian-oil-highlights-pragmatism-brics-push-for-dollar-alternatives-experts-say.html) · [cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/17/us-seeks-to-expand-energy-sales-to-india-why-it-could-be-a-problem-for-new-delhi)*

---

### China Cancels Taiwan President's Foreign Trip via Airspace Denial—First in History

On April 21, Taiwan's President William Lai Ching-te cancelled his entire diplomatic visit to Eswatini after Mauritius, Madagascar, and Seychelles revoked overflight permissions for his aircraft. It was the first time a Taiwanese president abandoned a foreign trip due to airspace denial—a coordinated gray-zone coercion that bypasses military action entirely. China has not publicly claimed credit, but the timing and coordination signal a new strategic playbook: isolating Taiwan through logistics and third-party pressure rather than kinetic escalation. The US, EU, France, Germany, and Britain all protested the airspace blockade within 48 hours.

**Why it matters.** This is how great-power competition erodes smaller states' autonomy. For Taiwan, it's a soft blockade that undermines the illusion of normal diplomatic engagement. For you as someone tracking geopolitical risk: this matters because Taiwan manufactures 90% of advanced semiconductor production. Every uptick in cross-strait coercion feeds into semiconductor supply-chain premiums and capex volatility for foundries like TSMC—risk that spills directly into your consulting clients' cost models if they touch chip supply. The playbook also signals China's confidence that the US won't escalate: prediction markets show only 7.5% implied probability of invasion by December 2026, suggesting Beijing views gray-zone pressure as safer than force.

**Action.** Watch TSMC earnings calls for supply-chain rhetoric and capex guidance into 2027. Read AEI's April 24 China-Taiwan update for the military exercise tempo; routine gray-zone ops are noise, but a marked acceleration would signal shifting risk. Track cross-strait relations through Taiwan's foreign ministry statements and any further denied overflights to allied nations—this is the new playbook.

*Sources: [usnews.com](https://www.usnews.com/news/world/articles/2026-04-25/taiwan-foreign-minister-arrives-in-eswatini-after-presidents-trip-blocked) · [aei.org](https://www.aei.org/articles/china-taiwan-update-april-24-2026/) · [foreignaffairs.com](https://www.foreignaffairs.com/china/perfect-storm-taiwan-2026)*

## 🧠 Artificial Intelligence

### GPT-5.5 "Spud" Ships April 23 — First Full OpenAI Retrain Since GPT-5, Leads Terminal-Bench 2.0 at 82.7%

OpenAI released GPT-5.5 (internal codename "Spud") on April 23, 2026, marking the first fully retrained base model since GPT-5 — not a fine-tuned derivative or adapter stack. The model is natively omnimodal at the architecture level, processing text, images, audio, and video in a single unified forward pass rather than routing to specialized heads. On SWE-Bench Verified it scores 88.7%, on Terminal-Bench 2.0 it scores 82.7% — leading Claude Opus 4.7 (69.4%) by 13.3 points — and it cuts output token count by roughly 40% versus GPT-5.4 on identical Codex tasks.

**Why it matters.** The Terminal-Bench 2.0 gap is the number that matters most for teams building agentic systems: Terminal-Bench measures long-horizon shell tasks requiring multi-tool coordination, so a 13-point lead over the next competitor is not noise. If you are orchestrating agents that need to browse, execute, debug, and iterate without human checkpoints, GPT-5.5 is now the only model that has shown it can sustain that loop reliably at benchmark scale. The token efficiency improvement is not cosmetic either — 40% fewer output tokens on the same Codex tasks means your agentic scaffolding costs drop materially, which changes the economics of running automated code review or refactoring pipelines at continuous-integration frequency. The native omnimodal architecture also eliminates the latency and error-surface of routing audio/video inputs through a separate pipeline.

**Action.** Pull the GPT-5.5 system card from OpenAI's deployment safety hub before rolling it into production agents — it describes the preparedness evaluations and the conditions under which the model was cleared. Then run your own Terminal-Bench suite on your current agent harness and compare: if you are on GPT-5.4 or Claude Opus 4.7, this is the clearest empirical reason to re-evaluate your model choice. If you are price-sensitive, benchmark token-count delta on your actual workload before committing; GPT-5.5 carries a higher list price than GPT-5.4, but the 40% efficiency gain may net out positive.

*Sources: [openai.com](https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-5-5/) · [venturebeat.com](https://venturebeat.com/ai/openais-gpt-5-5-is-here-and-its-no-potato-narrowly-beats-anthropics-claude-mythos-preview-on-terminal-bench-2-0) · [tokenmix.ai](https://tokenmix.ai/blog/gpt-5-5-spud-review-88-swe-bench-2026)*

---

### DeepSeek V4 Preview Drops 1.6T-Parameter MoE with 128x KV Compression — $1.74/M Input, Open Weights

DeepSeek released preview weights for DeepSeek-V4-Pro and DeepSeek-V4-Flash on April 24, 2026, one day after GPT-5.5. V4-Pro is a Mixture-of-Experts model with 1.6 trillion total parameters and 49 billion activated per token; V4-Flash runs 284B total with 13B activated. Both support a one-million-token context window. At that context length, V4-Pro requires only 10% of the KV cache memory of DeepSeek-V3.2 — the 2025 model that itself shocked the industry on efficiency — achieved through a two-tier hybrid attention scheme called Compressed Sparse Attention (4x sequence compression via softmax-gated pooling) and Heavily Compressed Attention (128x compression, dense attention over compressed blocks). V4-Pro scores 80.6% on SWE-Bench Verified, within 0.2 points of Claude Opus 4.6.

**Why it matters.** The KV cache number is the architectural story. Running a 1M-token context on V4-Pro requires 2% of the KV cache of a standard GQA-8 bfloat16 model — this means long-context serving, which has been the most expensive part of deploying MoE at scale, is now plausible on fewer GPUs. If you are building retrieval-augmented pipelines that need to condition on very large codebases or document corpora in-context rather than chunking, V4-Pro's 1M-token window is now economically viable rather than a theoretical capability. DeepSeek also switched from AdamW to the Muon optimizer at this scale and introduced "Anticipatory Routing" (routing network updates on historical backbone parameters) to prevent the training spikes that have destabilized previous large MoE runs. The model runs on Huawei Ascend 950 clusters, not NVIDIA, which is strategically significant for anyone modeling China's AI export-control resilience. API pricing: V4-Pro at $1.74/M input and $3.48/M output is a fraction of GPT-5.5's list.

**Action.** Pull the weights from Hugging Face (deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro) and run the vLLM-optimized serving path — the vLLM team published a blog post specifically covering V4's hybrid attention kernels. If you are currently chunking documents to fit within 128K context windows, benchmark a full-document retrieval run against your chunked baseline: the 1M window may let you drop your RAG (retrieval-augmented generation) layer for many workloads and simplify your pipeline significantly. Read the technical report on CSA/HCA before adopting the architecture in your own long-context work.

*Sources: [api-docs.deepseek.com](https://api-docs.deepseek.com/news/news260424) · [techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/24/deepseek-previews-new-ai-model-that-closes-the-gap-with-frontier-models/) · [huggingface.co](https://huggingface.co/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro)*

## 🇮🇳 India

### Oolka raises ₹130 crore Series A to automate credit underwriting for MSMEs across India

Oolka, an agentic AI credit management platform founded by ex-Meesho executive Utkrishta Kumar, secured ₹130 crore ($14.7 million) in Series A funding led by Accel India on April 24, 2026. The round values the company at ₹730 crore (~$87.6 million) post-money and includes participation from Lightspeed India, Z47, and personal investments from Meesho co-founders Vidit Aatrey and Sanjeev Barnwal. The platform has already scaled to 2 million users and processed over ₹100 crore in credit repayments.

**Why it matters.** For AI engineers building in India's fintech space, Oolka demonstrates proven product-market fit in credit automation—the hardest part of the lending stack for MSMEs. The raise validates that generative AI isn't speculative here; it's moving credit decisioning at scale. If you're considering roles at fintech platforms or building your own credit-tech product, you're watching the market move from rule engines to agentic AI handling underwriting and recovery—exactly the engineering problems that draw senior talent and venture capital right now.

**Action.** Track Oolka's tech stack and hiring (likely looking for ML/backend engineers specializing in credit risk); pull their announcements on which LLMs power their underwriting agents; watch Accel's fintech thesis since Lightspeed's double-down suggests validated repeat theses across VCs. Relevant for anyone evaluating fintech AI vs. broader deeptech opportunities.

*Sources: [[YourStory](https://yourstory.com/2026/04/oolka-raises-rs-130-cr-in-series-a-funding-round-led-by-accel)]([YourStory](https://yourstory.com/2026/04/oolka-raises-rs-130-cr-in-series-a-funding-round-led-by-accel)) · [[Entrackr](https://entrackr.com/exclusive/exclusive-oolka-raises-rs-130-cr-in-series-a-led-by-accel-at-rs-730-cr-valuation-11760159)]([Entrackr](https://entrackr.com/exclusive/exclusive-oolka-raises-rs-130-cr-in-series-a-led-by-accel-at-rs-730-cr-valuation-11760159)) · [[Startup Talky](https://startuptalky.com/news/daily-indian-funding-roundup-key-news-24-april-2026-oolka-raises-130-crore-playsimple-files-3150-crore-ipo/)]([Startup Talky](https://startuptalky.com/news/daily-indian-funding-roundup-key-news-24-april-2026-oolka-raises-130-crore-playsimple-files-3150-crore-ipo/))*

### KRAFTON and Naver back ₹6,000 crore Unicorn Growth Fund targeting Indian AI and deeptech founders

South Korean tech giants KRAFTON and Naver Corporation launched the Unicorn Growth Fund with Mirae Asset Venture Investments on April 21, 2026—a ₹6,000 crore (~$720 million) investment vehicle exclusively for Indian technology companies. The fund will target four sectors: AI and software platforms, consumer internet, semiconductors, robotics, and advanced materials. Portfolio companies gain direct access to product expertise, AI infrastructure, and market access into South Korea and wider Asia.

**Why it matters.** This is the single largest capital pool backing Indian deeptech in 2026, and it removes geography friction for Indian founders. If you're exploring generative AI infrastructure plays, semiconductor design, robotics, or advanced materials—the exact areas where India can compete globally—you now have capital from two $100B+ Asian tech ecosystems backing you. The fund's focus on "category-leading companies with global ambition" signals this isn't incremental; you can build at the scale of Open Source LLMs or edge AI hardware, not just SaaS margins.

**Action.** Monitor MAVI's (Mirae Asset Venture Investments) investment thesis and portfolio updates; map which portfolio companies KRAFTON and Naver route into their platforms; for deeptech founders, this becomes a must-track fund for Series B conversations. If you're considering whether to build AI infrastructure in India vs. relocating, this capital rebalances the equation.

*Sources: [[ANI News](https://aninews.in/news/business/krafton-and-naver-partner-with-mirae-asset-venture-investments-to-launch-inr-6000cr-india-focused-unicorn-growth-fund20260421144259/)]([ANI News](https://aninews.in/news/business/krafton-and-naver-partner-with-mirae-asset-venture-investments-to-launch-inr-6000cr-india-focused-unicorn-growth-fund20260421144259/)) · [[The Tech Portal](https://thetechportal.com/2026/04/23/krafton-naver-and-mirae-asset-announce-%E2%82%B96000-crore-india-focused-unicorn-growth-fund/)]([The Tech Portal](https://thetechportal.com/2026/04/23/krafton-naver-and-mirae-asset-announce-%E2%82%B96000-crore-india-focused-unicorn-growth-fund/)) · [[Seoul Economic Daily](https://en.sedaily.com/technology/2026/04/21/naver-krafton-mirae-asset-join-forces-for-major-tech)]([Seoul Economic Daily](https://en.sedaily.com/technology/2026/04/21/naver-krafton-mirae-asset-join-forces-for-major-tech))*

## 🏗 Tech & Startups

### Oolka Raises ₹130 Crore Series A for AI-Powered Credit Platform

Oolka, an AI-driven credit management platform targeting underserved borrowers, closed a ₹130 crore ($14 million) Series A led by Accel Partners. The round included participation from Lightspeed Venture Partners, Z47, and Meesho co-founders, signaling strong confidence in the fintech's approach to streamlining credit underwriting and recovery for MSMEs and new-to-credit borrowers.

**Why it matters.** This deal demonstrates investor appetite for fintech infrastructure solving real friction in emerging credit markets. Rather than chasing high-volume consumer lending, Oolka targets credit underwriting efficiency and collection automation—sectors where AI integration directly impacts default rates and unit economics. The Meesho co-founder backing validates an insider understanding of India's MSME credit gap.

**Action.** Track Oolka's path to profitability and CAC dynamics in a competitive space with BharatPe and mPokket. Request a deck on their AI-powered scorecard methodology and check LinkedIn for hiring patterns showing expansion into specific segments.

*Sources: [[Bhavya Sharma & Associates Indian Startup Funding Roundup](https://www.bhavyasharmaandassociates.com/indian-startup-funding-roundup-april-26-2026/)]([Bhavya Sharma & Associates Indian Startup Funding Roundup](https://www.bhavyasharmaandassociates.com/indian-startup-funding-roundup-april-26-2026/)) · [[Inc42 Deep Tech Coverage](https://inc42.com/industry/deep-tech/)]([Inc42 Deep Tech Coverage](https://inc42.com/industry/deep-tech/))*

### KreditBee Becomes India's Newest Unicorn on $1 Billion Valuation

Consumer lending platform KreditBee crossed the $1 billion valuation threshold in April 2026, joining India's exclusive unicorn club. The Bengaluru fintech serves over 10 million customers with personal and salary advance loans, demonstrating the scale required for unicorn status in India's crowded lending market.

**Why it matters.** KreditBee's ascension reflects the consolidation of fintech winners in personal lending—a market where unit economics, customer retention, and defaults become the deciding factor, not growth velocity. Reaching $1B valuation with a narrower product focus than mega-round competitors signals investor confidence in profitability-first playbooks in mature fintech verticals.

**Action.** Monitor KreditBee's path to IPO and capital deployment strategy post-unicorn status. Review their recent earnings or founder blog for net revenue retention and loan loss provisioning metrics, which determine fintech durability.

*Sources: [[Bhavya Sharma & Associates Funding Roundup](https://www.bhavyasharmaandassociates.com/indian-startup-funding-roundup-april-26-2026/)]([Bhavya Sharma & Associates Funding Roundup](https://www.bhavyasharmaandassociates.com/indian-startup-funding-roundup-april-26-2026/)) · [[Tracxn India Startup Data](https://tracxn.com/d/geographies/india/)]([Tracxn India Startup Data](https://tracxn.com/d/geographies/india/))*

## 🩹 Health & Science

### Fish Oil May Impair Brain Recovery After Repeated Head Injuries

Medical University of South Carolina researchers discovered that EPA, a key omega-3 fatty acid abundant in fish oil supplements, interferes with the brain's repair mechanisms after repetitive mild traumatic brain injury. Published March 25, 2026 in Cell Reports, the study found that animals receiving EPA showed weaker neurological recovery, impaired spatial learning, and perivascular tau accumulation—hallmarks of chronic traumatic encephalopathy (CTE). EPA disrupted endothelial repair functions while sparing DHA, a related omega-3 that remained protective.

**Why it matters.** This reverses conventional wisdom on fish oil supplementation and creates immediate personal-health guidance for a vulnerable population. Athletes, military personnel, and anyone with repeated concussion history are commonly advised to take fish oil for neuroprotection—this finding suggests EPA specifically may be harmful in that context. The mechanistic insight into vascular disruption and tau pathology offers a testable hypothesis for why some head-injured individuals develop accelerated cognitive decline, opening doors to targeted interventions that preserve DHA benefits while blocking EPA's deleterious effects.

**Action.** Clinicians should revise supplement guidance for patients with history of repeated head injuries: prioritize DHA-only formulations or whole-fish sources (which contain balanced ratios), and consider EPA avoidance in this cohort. The tau-vascular connection warrants follow-up studies in human TBI cohorts and potentially justifies clinical trials of EPA-blocking strategies in at-risk populations.

*Sources: [[ScienceDaily](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2026/04/260426012303.htm)]([ScienceDaily](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2026/04/260426012303.htm)) · [[Medical Express](https://medicalxpress.com/news/2026-04-widespread-belief-fish-oil-effects.html)]([Medical Express](https://medicalxpress.com/news/2026-04-widespread-belief-fish-oil-effects.html)) · [[Cell Reports](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5531869/)]([Cell Reports](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5531869/))*

---

### Graphene Oxide Selectively Kills Antibiotic-Resistant Bacteria While Sparing Human Cells

Researchers at Korea Advanced Institute of Science and Technology (KAIST) identified the precise mechanism by which graphene oxide destroys drug-resistant superbugs: its oxygen functional groups bind exclusively to POPG phospholipids found only in bacterial membranes, leaving human cell membranes untouched. Published April 24, 2026 in Advanced Functional Materials, the mechanism explains why graphene-nanofiber materials kill multidrug-resistant bacteria while maintaining biocompatibility. Commercial validation has already arrived—graphene antibacterial toothbrushes based on this technology have sold over 10 million units globally.

**Why it matters.** This solves a 15-year materials-science challenge: selective bacterial killing without toxicity to host cells. With antibiotic resistance claiming 1.3 million deaths annually and accelerating, a material that works against resistant pathogens without triggering resistance itself represents genuine inflection. The selective mechanism (targeting bacterial-exclusive lipids) is harder to circumvent than small-molecule antibiotics and scales to surfaces, wound dressings, and implants—creating multiple commercial pathways for biotech and medical-device companies.

**Action.** Biotech founders should evaluate graphene-oxide formulations for wound care, orthopedic implant coatings, and hospital-acquired infection prevention. The POPG-targeting principle suggests opportunities to engineer graphene variants with tuned selectivity toward specific pathogenic strains. Patent landscape around KAIST's discovery (Materials Creation Co., Ltd. spinout) should inform IP strategy.

*Sources: [[ScienceDaily](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2026/04/260424233210.htm)]([ScienceDaily](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2026/04/260424233210.htm)) · [[Phys.org](https://phys.org/news/2026-03-graphene-oxide-bacteria-human-cells.html)]([Phys.org](https://phys.org/news/2026-03-graphene-oxide-bacteria-human-cells.html)) · [[Graphene-Info](https://www.graphene-info.com/researchers-examine-how-graphene-oxide-selectively-kills-bacteria-while)]([Graphene-Info](https://www.graphene-info.com/researchers-examine-how-graphene-oxide-selectively-kills-bacteria-while))*

## 🎤 Culture & World

### Michael Jackson Biopic Sets Record with $97 Million Opening Weekend, Largest Ever for Biographical Film

Antoine Fuqua's "Michael" premiered April 24, 2026, starring Jaafar Jackson (Michael's nephew, in his film debut) as the King of Pop. The film grossed $39.5 million on its first day, surpassing Oppenheimer for the biggest opening day ever for a biopic, and went on to $97 million for the weekend — the highest opening weekend for any biographical film in history. The film chronicles Jackson's life from the Jackson 5 era through the Bad tour of the late 1980s, with a supporting cast including Laura Harrier, Miles Teller, and Colman Domingo.

**Why it matters.** The box office performance signals that Jackson's cultural magnetism transcends his death and controversies, and that audiences — particularly Gen Z discovering his catalog via streaming — still see his narrative as central to pop music history. However, the 38% Rotten Tomatoes consensus reveals the challenge: critics note the film functions as a "greatest hits" album without deeper insight. More structurally, legal settlements forced removal of Jackson's 1993 child sexual abuse allegations and substantial third-act reshoots, revealing ongoing disputes over narrative control around contested figures in streaming-era pop culture.

**Action.** Track whether "Michael" sustains internationally (it's getting global releases) and whether the box office trajectory holds against streaming cannibalisation. The critical gap between audience reception and reviews signals a generational split worth monitoring — what draws Gen Z to this version of Jackson versus what older audiences expect from biopic storytelling.

*Sources: [[IMDB](https://www.imdb.com/title/tt11378946/)]([IMDB](https://www.imdb.com/title/tt11378946/)) · [[CNN](https://www.cnn.com/2026/04/22/entertainment/michael-jackson-biopic)]([CNN](https://www.cnn.com/2026/04/22/entertainment/michael-jackson-biopic)) · [[Rotten Tomatoes](https://www.rottentomatoes.com/m/michael)]([Rotten Tomatoes](https://www.rottentomatoes.com/m/michael))*

---

### The Weeknd Performs Sold-Out Rio Stadium Show as Streaming-Era Trilogy Tour Scales Across Latin America

The Weeknd played Estádio Nilton Santos in Rio de Janeiro on April 26, 2026, as part of the "After Hours Til Dawn Stadium Tour," which celebrates his trilogy of streaming albums: After Hours (2020), Dawn FM (2022), and Hurry Up Tomorrow (2025). Anitta opened the show and also appears on "São Paulo" from Hurry Up Tomorrow. The 43,810-capacity venue sold out, marking the tour's only Brazil-specific Carioca date on a global run already boasting over 40 sold-out US stadium dates.

**Why it matters.** The tour structure — binned around a three-album arc released across streaming platforms — demonstrates how artists now design stadium tours around algorithmic release calendars rather than single-album cycles. Latin America's significance for R&B/hip-hop expansion (paired with Anitta as local anchor) signals streaming platforms' geographic priorities. The tour's scale and performer economics suggest stadium touring has stabilized as the post-pandemic revenue floor for major streaming artists.

**Action.** Watch the tour's international box office performance and whether platforms like Spotify/Apple Music use the streaming trilogy narrative in marketing the shows. The Anitta collaboration thread is worth tracking as a signal of cross-regional artist dependencies in the streaming era.

*Sources: [[Live Nation Brasil](https://www.livenation.com.br/en/event/the-weeknd-after-hours-til-dawn-stadium-tour-rio-de-janeiro-tickets-edp1618425)]([Live Nation Brasil](https://www.livenation.com.br/en/event/the-weeknd-after-hours-til-dawn-stadium-tour-rio-de-janeiro-tickets-edp1618425)) · [[Songkick](https://www.songkick.com/concerts/42774388-weeknd-at-estadio-olimpico-nilton-santos)]([Songkick](https://www.songkick.com/concerts/42774388-weeknd-at-estadio-olimpico-nilton-santos)) · [[Rio Times](https://www.riotimesonline.com/rio-de-janeiro-daily-brief-for-sunday-april-26-2026/)]([Rio Times](https://www.riotimesonline.com/rio-de-janeiro-daily-brief-for-sunday-april-26-2026/))*
